task_completion.description: Agent accesses the Nx Witness REST API
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the agent using a browser to access the Nx Witness API documentation/Swagger UI at https://localhost:7001, or preparing a terminal command (curl) or script (Python) targeting this URL.

task_completion.description: Agent retrieves the ID for the 'Lobby Camera'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A visible API request (and response) querying the system's devices/cameras to find the unique identifier (UUID) associated with the 'Lobby Camera'.

task_completion.description: Agent constructs a layout creation request with the correct name
task_completion.visual_evidence: A JSON payload or API request body being drafted or sent that includes the layout name 'Lobby POS Monitor'.

task_completion.description: Agent includes two views of the Lobby Camera in the layout
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layout creation payload contains an array of items/resources where the Lobby Camera's ID is referenced exactly twice, representing the side-by-side views.

task_completion.description: Agent configures the digital zoom ROI for the second view
task_completion.visual_evidence: The payload specifies zoom or item parameters for one of the camera views with the exact coordinates: x=0.5, y=0.5, width=0.5, height=0.5 (or w=0.5, h=0.5).

task_completion.description: Agent successfully executes the layout creation request
task_completion.visual_evidence: The terminal, script output, or API client shows a successful HTTP response (e.g., 200 OK, or a JSON response containing the new layout details) after the creation request is sent.

integrity.description: Agent used the REST API instead of the Desktop GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must accomplish the task using API requests (via curl, scripts, or browser-based API UI). The agent should NOT be seen opening the Nx Witness Desktop client to manually draw the zoom region or create the layout.

integrity.description: Agent actually sent the API request to the server
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent executes the request and receives a genuine server response, rather than just typing a JSON payload into a text editor without running it.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the specified local system
integrity.visual_evidence: API requests are explicitly directed to https://localhost:7001, proving interaction with the correct test environment.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a layout named 'Lobby POS Monitor' containing two views of 'Lobby Camera' via the Nx Witness REST API at https://localhost:7001. The second view must have a digital zoom configured with coordinates x=0.5, y=0.5, width=0.5, height=0.5 (representing the bottom-right quadrant). Look for API requests (via curl, Python, or API docs UI) containing these exact names and coordinate values, followed by a successful API response.
